title: Sacramento State University Afghan Student Association 2023 Banquet Attan
body:
Wow. Amazing display of Attan. This is quite common in the Pashtoon belts, both in Afghanistan and Pakistan. Pashtoons in Pakistan, especially in South Khyber Pukhtunkhwa and north Baluchistan are very fond of staging Attan every now and then on occasions like weddings etc.

I m particularly impressed of the rythem and perfection of the boys and girls in the dance. Wonderful indeed. Long live Pashtoons, both in Afghanistan and Pakistan.
author: @NawabKhan-kq8kc

title: Everything You Need to Know About the CSU Schools
body: These videos are great! I'm currently preparing for applications next semester so this content is excellent for an overview of all the countless California colleges. There are few YouTubers who go in-depth about the UCs and CSUs so it's nice to see someone finally covering it. Keep it up!
author: @charityspicer4462

title: Everything You Need to Know About the CSU Schools
body: Excellent video! I am on path to become a transfer student at Cal State San Bernardino, a school I've had my eye on for years. Since I am going into Computer Science, CSU San Bernardino has a great computer engineering program alongside Cal State Long Beach, San Jose State, and the Poly's. Compared to every other state in the US, I personally think California has the best state-wide public school system in the nation, since they make it extremely affordable and also accessible in big cities to small towns, like where I'm from.
author: @lukedstaten

title: Everything You Need to Know About the CSU Schools
body: Any indigenous CSU students get free schooling. Your tribe doesn’t need to be located in California. However, you will need to be a citizen of a federally recognized tribe. Just want to let you all know if you didn’t already. Happy schooling and I hope you all do great!
author: @Toneychaplin

title: Everything You Need to Know About the CSU Schools
body: Current CSUF grad that received full rides through academic scholarships. Was able to achieve a 3 year graduation from high school and land a 6 figure job out of a business undergrad. Highly recommend the CSU track for strong students too.

title: Everything You Need to Know About the CSU Schools
body: I graduated from Cal Poly SLO back in the early 1980’s with a Bachelor of Architecture degree. Although the professors in the Architecture and Engineering departments were top notch, I had some instructors in General Ed courses who were not very good. Before transferring into Cal Poly, I went to Pasadena City College, which back then, was the second best rated community college in the state. Many of the instructors I had for General Ed courses and others like math and science courses, were far superior to those at Cal Poly. Fortunately, I had completed the majority of my General Ed courses at Pasadena City College. PCC also had a good architecture department, so when applying to transfer to Cal Poly SLO school of Architecture, I did not have to submit a portfolio of my work like those from other community colleges due to the architecture program at Cal Poly being impacted at that time. I think to this day, the program is still impacted and it is difficult to get accepted to the program.

body: as someone who’s in CPP majoring in VCD,  best campus, major diversity, and spoke with many alumnus about how much opportunities they got from there department. I’m taking 15 units with two part time jobs on campus as a graphic designer for the cultural centers and also a student fundraiser and I’m on a scholarship, so literally it may be hell for me, but the people around me make it the best moments. look into each cal state & see scholarship opportunities and look into research as well!!

title: Sac State OFFICIAL Campus Tour
body: Spent my last two and a half years of college there, graduating in 1983 -- yes, 43 years ago. Couldn't stand the place. Walked out of there after my last class ended, got into my loaded-up pickup truck, and drove home; had them mail my diploma. Have not been back since, and never will.
